title: soap-sbar-llm-benchmark: Quantifying Safety-Critical Information Loss in LLM-Generated Clinical Handoffs (Data, Code, and Materials), v1.2.0
description:
Reproducibility package for a priority-stratified, three-stage automated evaluation pipeline that quantifies safety-critical omission (SCO) — the proportion of high-priority clinical facts completely omitted — when four commercial large language models translate Japanese SOAP nursing records into SBAR handoff summaries.

Models evaluated

  • GPT-4o (gpt-4o-2024-08-06)
  • GPT-5.5 (gpt-5.5-2026-04-23)
  • Claude Opus 4.5 (claude-opus-4-5-20251101)
  • Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite (gemini-2.5-flash-lite)

Contents

  • 9 source SOAP scenarios, authored de novo across acute, chronic, and palliative-care categories.
  • 180 pre-specified fact tags annotated with content domain, clinical priority (high 88 / medium 66 / low 26), target SBAR section, and preservation difficulty (easy 88 / medium 74 / hard 18).
  • 2,160 generated SBAR records (4 models × 9 scenarios × 60 trials; temperatures 0.0 and 1.0).
  • Three-stage automated evaluation pipeline: deterministic Stage 1 + paired cross-vendor AI judges (GPT-4o and Gemini) Stage 2 + arbiter (Claude Haiku 4.5) Stage 3, with gap-fixed consensus scores.
  • Independent second-rater reliability validation: a registered nurse, independent of and blinded to the author, re-scored a stratified 400-cell subset and independently re-assigned all 180 priority labels.
  • Statistical analysis scripts (Friedman, Wilcoxon signed-rank, Cohen's κ).
  • Pinned dependency versions in requirements.txt.

Headline result

Safety-critical omission of high-priority facts: GPT-4o 37.7% (95% CI 28.4–45.3), GPT-5.5 19.4% (12.8–24.6), Claude Opus 4.5 17.0% (11.7–23.7), Gemini 2.5 Flash-Lite 17.0% (9.3–22.4). The three current-generation models are statistically indistinguishable; the earlier-generation GPT-4o omits significantly more (Friedman χ²(3) = 13.58, p = 0.0035; Kendall's W = 0.503). Inter-rater agreement between the two clinicians: Cohen's κ = 0.933 (95% CI 0.901–0.961); priority-label agreement κ = 0.973 (95% CI 0.937–1.000).

Changes in v1.2.0

Major revision: the study is reframed around safety-critical omission, a fourth model (GPT-5.5) is added, the corpus is expanded to 2,160 SBAR records, a gap-fixed high-priority-tag analysis and an independent second-rater reliability validation are added, and the documentation/metadata are aligned with the substantially revised manuscript. See CHANGELOG.md and RELEASE_NOTES_v1.2.0.md in the repository.

Linked manuscript

Tajima H. Quantifying Safety-Critical Information Loss in Large Language Model-Generated Clinical Handoffs: A Priority-Stratified Automated Evaluation Pipeline. 2026; under review.

notes: v1.2.0 reframes the study around safety-critical omission, adds a fourth model (GPT-5.5) for a total of 2,160 SBAR records, adds a gap-fixed high-priority-tag analysis and an independent second-rater reliability validation (Cohen's kappa = 0.933 inter-rater; 0.973 for priority labels), and aligns documentation and metadata with the revised manuscript. See CHANGELOG.md and RELEASE_NOTES_v1.2.0.md in the repository for the complete change log.
